metadata_information.title: More Benefits, Fewer Children: How Regularization Affects Immigrant Fertility
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: More Benefits, Fewer Children: How Regularization Affects Immigrant Fertility

document_description.abstract: How do policies that ease the integration of immigrants shape their fertility decisions? This paper uses a panel survey of undocumented Venezuelan migrants in Colombia to compare the fertility decisions of households before and after the launch of an amnesty program that granted such migrants a labor permit and access to social services. The results suggest the amnesty reduced the likelihood that program beneficiaries would have a child due to better labor market opportunities for women and greater access to family planning resources through health care services.
